triageFlags.title: Loan Term Exceeds Maximum Allowed
triageFlags.description: The loan term exceeds the maximum allowable term of 60 months as per policy.
triageFlags.recommendation: Adjust the loan term to comply with the maximum allowable term of 60 months.
triageFlags.reasoning: The policy specifies a maximum term of 60 months for any agreement, and the current term of 72 months exceeds this limit, posing a potential risk.

triageFlags.title: Loan-to-Value Ratio Exceeds Policy Limit
triageFlags.description: The Loan-to-Value (LTV) ratio exceeds the maximum allowed for the applicant's credit tier.
triageFlags.recommendation: Consider reducing the loan amount or increasing the deposit to bring the LTV ratio within the acceptable range.
triageFlags.reasoning: The policy allows a maximum LTV of 100% for Tier B applicants, and the current LTV of 91.11% is slightly above this threshold, indicating a need for adjustment.

content.reasoning: The identified issues, while not critical, indicate a need for adjustments to ensure compliance with policy guidelines and mitigate potential risks.
